rationale.root_cause: They pinpointed the unsafe strcpy at program.c:11 copying argc[1] into buf[256] and explained that a long command-line argument (attacker-controlled argv data) can exceed the destination, causing a global-buffer-overflow. This matches the expected bug location and failure mode.
rationale.local_fix: They primarily recommended snprintf (truncation), not a fix that rejects overlong input before copying as required. Although they mentioned a length check as an alternative, the clearly recommended fix does not perform rejection.
rationale.global_fix: They proposed eliminating unbounded string APIs (strcpy/strcat/sprintf), using capacity-aware/bounded APIs or dynamic allocation, and instituting codebase-wide bans/tooling, which removes raw unbounded string handling from the data path. This satisfies the root-cause/global remedy.
